plan.title: VREd Sprint 1: Chemistry VR Platform Integration
plan.overview: Integrate the PSE-in-VR (Babylon.js) chemistry education app into the Mozilla Hubs VR platform. Build the bridge between two codebases through Spoke scene tools, Reticulum room types, and CORS-secured iframe embedding.

opportunity_1.title: PSE-in-VR ↔ Hubs Integration Bridge
opportunity_1.value: The single highest-value move. PSE-in-VR has 118 element rooms, 12 themes, 10 experimental rooms in Babylon.js. Hubs has social VR infrastructure. The integration creates a chemistry classroom where students explore elements socially. This is unique — no competitor has a live chemistry VR deployment.
opportunity_1.risk: Medium. CORS, iframe communication, auth flow complexity.

opportunity_2.title: CI/CD & Production Hardening
opportunity_2.value: Currently deploys from a dev docker-compose with mutagen sync. Moving to CI/CD with Forgejo runner gives deployment confidence, rollback capability, and enables the team to move fast without fear.
opportunity_2.risk: Low. Mostly config work. Highest ROI per hour spent.

opportunity_3.title: Authorization & Room Access Control
opportunity_3.value: Chemistry education needs per-classroom access control. Currently Hubs rooms are link-share. Adding JWT-based room auth via Reticulum + Dialog enables teacher-controlled classrooms.
opportunity_3.risk: High. Elixir deep-dive, security auditing, breakage risk.

plan.recommendation: Priority 1: Integration Bridge (opportunity 1). Priority 2: CI/CD (opportunity 2). Priority 3: Auth (opportunity 3 — defer to next sprint). Rationale: Integration delivers immediate differentiated value. CI/CD enables safe iteration. Auth is high-value but high-risk and should follow after infra is solid.
architecture_decisions: AD-001: Use iframe media-frame embedding (not custom Hubs client fork) for PSE integration. Prevents divergence from upstream Hubs.
AD-002: Use Reticulum URL params for room-to-element mapping. Element symbol passed as ?element=Na in iframe src.
AD-003: CORS proxy in Reticulum (not a separate service) to minimize operational surface.
AD-004: Auth integration via Dialog JWT (Hubs' existing WebRTC auth flow), not custom Elixir auth module.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Babylon.js content rendered inside Hubs 3D space has different interaction model
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Media frame approach keeps PSE as independent app. Users 'enter' PSE via link/embed, not inline.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: CORS restrictions prevent cross-origin iframe communication
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Reticulum CORS proxy route + PostgREST API for element data. No direct cross-origin JS needed.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Upstream Hubs changes could conflict with customizations
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: AD-001: iframe embedding requires zero Hubs client modifications. Room type is a Reticulum data change only.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Dialog certs managed in docker-compose.yml outside Reticulum config
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Consolidate cert paths into Reticulum config.exs. Single source of truth for all service auth.

codons.title: Spoke: Measurement Tool (Position/Rotation/Scale Panel)
codons.description: Build a measurement tool in Spoke that shows position, rotation, and scale of selected 3D objects in a floating panel. This is the minimal verifiable piece that proves Spoke extensibility and gives educators precise scene control.
codons.commit_message: feat(spoke): add measurement tool showing position, rotation, scale of selected objects
tdd_steps: 1. Write test: mount component, verify it renders without selection
2. Write test: select an object, verify panel shows numeric values
3. Write test: deselect, verify panel shows 'no selection' state
4. Write test: verify values update when object is moved via transform controls
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Spoke loads with 📐 toggle button in toolbar
AC2: Clicking 📐 toggles measurement panel visibility
AC3: Selecting a 3D object shows: Position (x, y, z), Rotation (x, y, z in degrees), Scale (x, y, z)
AC4: Deselecting shows 'No object selected'
AC5: Values update in real-time when object is manipulated

steps: 6. Click the box to select it
7. Assert: panel shows Position, Rotation, Scale with numeric values

codons.title: Reticulum: Dialog Certs Consolidation
codons.description: Move Dialog certificate paths from docker-compose.yml environment variables into Reticulum's config.exs. This consolidates all auth-related configuration into the Elixir config where it belongs.
codons.commit_message: chore(reticulum): consolidate Dialog certificate config into reticulum config.exs
tdd_steps: 1. Verify: docker-compose config -q passes after changes
2. Verify: reticulum DNS resolution for dialog container
3. Verify: dialog healthcheck passes with new cert paths
acceptance_criteria: AC1: docker-compose.yml no longer has DIALOG_CERT_* env vars in dialog service
AC2: reticulum config.exs has dialog_cert_path and dialog_key_path
AC3: All services restart cleanly
AC4: Dialog WebRTC connections still work

codons.title: Reticulum: PSE VR Room Type + CORS Proxy
codons.description: Add a PSE_VR room type to Reticulum's room model. When a user creates a PSE VR room in Hubs, the scene loads an iframe/media-frame pointing to pse.chemie-lernen.org with element symbol as URL parameter.
codons.commit_message: feat(reticulum): add PSE VR room type with CORS proxy for pse.chemie-lernen.org
tdd_steps: 1. Write Ecto migration: add room_type enum with 'pse_vr'
2. Write test: create room of type pse_vr via API
3. Write test: GET /api/v1/rooms/:id returns pse_vr room data
4. Write test: CORS proxy route returns proper Access-Control-Allow-Origin headers
5. Write test: CORS proxy blocks unauthorized origins
acceptance_criteria: AC1: POST /api/v1/rooms with type='pse_vr' succeeds
AC2: GET /api/v1/rooms/:id for pse_vr room returns pse_url with element param
AC3: CORS proxy endpoint returns 200 with proper CORS headers for pse.chemie-lernen.org
AC4: CORS proxy returns 403 for unauthorized origins
AC5: Creating PSE VR room from Hubs client shows iframe in media frame

steps: 1. Note: Must run against running reticulum
4. Assert: HTTP 403 or no CORS headers

codons.title: PSE-in-VR: Embed Mode + Element Room Linking
codons.description: Add an 'embed' mode to the Babylon.js app that accepts element symbol via URL parameter (?element=Na) and navigates directly to that element room. This makes the PSE app embeddable in Hubs rooms.
codons.commit_message: feat(pse-vr): add embed mode with element room URL param navigation
tdd_steps: 1. Write test: mount() with startRoom='embed' + element='Na' navigates to Natrium room
2. Write test: mount() with invalid element symbol falls back to Lobby
3. Write test: embed mode hides non-essential UI elements (navigation hints, fullscreen button)
4. Write test: CORS headers present on iframe communication
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Visiting https://pse.chemie-lernen.org?element=Na loads Natrium room directly
AC2: Visiting https://pse.chemie-lernen.org without params loads Lobby
AC3: Embed mode hides navigation UI elements
AC4: Parent frame can postMessage to communicate room transitions

steps: 2. Assert: Wasserstoff room loads with H title visible
3. Assert: no navigation hints visible (embed mode)

codons.title: Deploy: PSE-in-VR Docker Deployment via Traefik
codons.description: Ensure PSE-in-VR has production Docker deployment running under pse.chemie-lernen.org via the existing Traefik reverse proxy (network: traefik-public). The docker-compose.yml already exists with proper Traefik labels.
codons.commit_message: deploy(pse-vr): production deployment with Traefik reverse proxy
tdd_steps: 1. docker-compose build --verify
2. docker-compose up -d --verify port availability
3. curl https://pse.chemie-lernen.org/ --verify SSL termination
4. curl https://pse.chemie-lernen.org?element=Na --verify element routing
acceptance_criteria: AC1: docker-compose up -d succeeds with no port conflicts
AC2: https://pse.chemie-lernen.org loads PSE VR app with valid SSL
AC3: https://pse.chemie-lernen.org?element=Na loads Natrium room
AC4: Traefik dashboard shows pse.chemie-lernen.org router

codons.title: CI/CD: Full Pipeline for Hubs-Compose
codons.description: Build a complete CI pipeline on the Forgejo runner that validates docker-compose syntax, builds all service images, runs Reticulum Elixir tests, and supports deployment. Add commit message validation.
codons.commit_message: ci(hubs-compose): add full CI pipeline with build, test, lint stages
tdd_steps: 1. Push CI config to Codeberg branch
2. Assert: CI pipeline triggers and validates docker-compose syntax
3. Assert: CI runs Reticulum deps.get + compile + test
4. Assert: CI runs npm lint + build for hubs-client/spoke
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Forgejo Actions pipeline validates docker-compose syntax
AC2: Reticulum Elixir compilation passes
AC3: hubs-client lint + build passes
AC4: Spoke build passes
AC5: Pipeline completes under 15 minutes

steps: 5. Assert: CI fails with clear error message

codons.title: Integration Test: PSE Room → Embed → Element
codons.description: End-to-end integration test that: (1) creates a PSE VR room in Hubs, (2) visits the room, (3) verifies the iframe loads PSE with correct element. This validates the entire integration chain.
codons.commit_message: test(e2e): add PSE VR integration test covering room→embed→element flow
tdd_steps: 1. Write test: create PSE VR room via API
2. Write test: visit Hubs room URL
3. Write test: verify iframe loads PSE app with element
4. Write test: element room content matches expected element
acceptance_criteria: AC1: E2E test creates PSE VR room programmatically
AC2: Test navigates to Hubs room and finds iframe
AC3: Iframe src contains correct element URL parameter
AC4: Test passes in CI on every push

steps: 3. Assert: Page navigates to Hubs room URL
4. Assert: Iframe element found with src matching pattern

in_scope: PSE-in-VR ↔ Hubs integration (iframe embedding, CORS proxy, room type)
Spoke measurement tool (first Spoke extensibility feature)
not_in_scope: Mobile app or native VR (Quest standalone)
Multi-language support beyond existing German UI

rules: Every commit must pass: docker-compose config -q + relevant service tests
Types: feat, fix, chore, test, ci, refactor, docs, deploy
No mega-commits. If a codon is too big, split it into sub-codons.
Each commit must be independently revertible
commit_strategy.squash_policy: No squashing during sprint. Squash only on merge to main.

execution_order.reason: Infrastructure prep — cert consolidation cleans up config before room type work
Quick win — Spoke measurement tool is independent and showcases extensibility
CI/CD — enables safe iteration for all subsequent work
Core integration — PSE room type + CORS proxy in Reticulum
PSE embed mode — makes Babylon.js app embeddable
Integration test — validate the entire chain end-to-end
